description_long: This object is a detailed model of a NASA Space Shuttle orbiter, featuring a white fuselage with black thermal protection tiles along the nose, wing edges, and underbelly. The wings are delta-shaped and mid-mounted, with a slight upward dihedral and no visible winglets. The rear section houses three large main engines flanked by two smaller orbital maneuvering system (OMS) pods, and a vertical stabilizer with a distinctive swept-back design. The cockpit is located at the forward end, with multiple black-framed windows and a sharply pointed nose. The landing gear is partially visible, with a nose wheel and main gear doors on the underside. The surface appears smooth and hard, with a combination of matte and slightly glossy finishes.
description: A NASA Space Shuttle orbiter with a white body, black thermal tiles, delta wings, and three large rear engines. The cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple windows and a swept vertical tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the broad delta wings extending outward.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the cockpit, and the three main engines at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the three large main engines, two OMS pods, and the vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the fuselage, cockpit, and rear propulsion system.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing delta configuration with slight upward dihedral and no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted jet engines with two additional OMS pods.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single swept vertical stabilizer and no horizontal stabilizers visible.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with visible nose and main gear doors.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple black-framed windows.
